test_cases.name: happy_path_complete_blog_post
test_cases.scenario: Analyze a well-structured 1500-word blog post with all optional inputs provided (keywords, meta description, links, dates, competitor URLs) to validate complete audit workflow.

input.blog_post_content: SEO Content Auditor: Your Complete Guide to Blog Optimization. Search engine optimization is critical for online visibility. This comprehensive guide covers keyword density, meta descriptions, header structure, image alt-text, internal linking, external linking, URL slugs, schema markup, readability, and content freshness. Primary keyword 'SEO content audit' appears naturally throughout. We recommend analyzing your blog posts regularly. Header structure matters for both users and search engines. Images should always have descriptive alt-text. Internal links help distribute page authority. External links to authoritative sources build credibility. Your URL slug should include target keywords. Schema markup enables rich snippets. Readability affects user engagement. Fresh content ranks better. This post demonstrates all best practices. The average sentence length is appropriate. Word choice is clear and accessible. Paragraphs are well-organized. Images include descriptive alt-text. Internal links point to relevant pages. External links reference authoritative sources. The meta description is compelling. The URL slug is keyword-optimized. Schema markup is properly implemented. Content was published recently. This guide will help you optimize your blog posts effectively. Follow these recommendations for better SEO performance. Your content will rank higher in search results. User engagement will improve significantly. Conversion rates typically increase. Traffic from organic search grows substantially. Your competitors will notice the improvement. Analytics will show measurable gains. Long-term success requires consistent optimization. Regular audits help maintain quality. This process becomes easier with practice. Your team will develop expertise over time. Documentation helps scale the process. Training ensures consistency across posts. Metrics track improvement over time. Dashboards visualize performance trends. Reports communicate results to stakeholders. Recommendations prioritize high-impact changes. Implementation follows a clear roadmap. Testing validates improvements before launch. Monitoring continues after publication. Iteration refines the approach continuously.
input.meta_description: Learn how to audit your blog posts for SEO optimization. Complete guide covering keyword density, headers, links, readability, and more.

test_cases.expected_behavior: All eight phases execute successfully. Keyword analysis identifies 'SEO content audit' as primary keyword with 1.5-2.5% density. Meta description is validated as present and optimized. Header structure is analyzed for hierarchy and keyword placement. Image alt-text coverage is assessed. Internal and external links are audited for quality and distribution. Readability metrics are calculated with Flesch-Kincaid grade 8-10. Schema markup recommendations are provided. Content freshness shows recent publication and update. Overall SEO score is 75-90 (good to excellent). Improvement comparison shows metric deltas from previous audit.

test_cases.name: happy_path_minimal_inputs
test_cases.scenario: Analyze a blog post with only required input (content) and minimal optional inputs to validate graceful handling of sparse data.

input.blog_post_content: Keyword optimization is essential. We focus on keyword density and readability. This article covers best practices. Keywords should appear naturally. Readability matters for users. Search engines prefer clear content. Headers organize information. Links provide context. Images enhance understanding. Meta descriptions summarize content. URL slugs should be descriptive. Schema markup helps search engines. Fresh content ranks better. All these factors contribute to SEO success. Optimization requires attention to detail. Regular audits improve performance. This process is ongoing. Best practices evolve over time. Stay updated with algorithm changes. Monitor your metrics carefully. Adjust your strategy accordingly. Success comes from consistent effort. Quality content is fundamental. User experience is paramount. Technical SEO provides foundation. On-page optimization matters. Off-page signals help too. Backlinks build authority. Social signals indicate relevance. Engagement metrics show value. Conversion tracking measures success. Analytics reveal insights. Data drives decisions. Testing validates hypotheses. Iteration improves results. Continuous improvement is key.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill executes all phases with graceful degradation. Keyword analysis infers primary keywords from content frequency (no target keywords provided). Meta description audit flags as missing and generates recommendation. URL slug audit is skipped (no URL provided). Image alt-text audit is marked N/A (no images in content). Internal linking audit provides generic recommendations without specific URLs (no site inventory). External linking audit is marked N/A (no external links). Content freshness audit is incomplete (no dates provided). Overall SEO score reflects missing optimizations. Report notes which audits were skipped due to missing inputs.

test_cases.name: edge_case_short_content
test_cases.scenario: Analyze a blog post with exactly 100 words (minimum threshold) to test boundary condition handling.

input.blog_post_content: SEO optimization is important for online visibility. Keyword density affects search rankings. Meta descriptions summarize page content. Header structure organizes information logically. Internal links distribute page authority. External links build credibility. Readability improves user engagement. Schema markup enables rich snippets. Content freshness signals relevance. URL slugs should include keywords. Image alt-text improves accessibility. All these factors contribute to SEO success. Regular audits identify improvement opportunities. Optimization is an ongoing process. Quality content is fundamental.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill processes 100-word content successfully. Keyword analysis calculates density for provided keywords. Readability metrics are calculated with caveat that short content may not provide reliable baseline. All phases execute but some may note 'limited data' for certain assessments. Overall SEO score reflects minimal content length. Report notes that content should be expanded to 300+ words for comprehensive analysis.

test_cases.name: edge_case_unicode_and_special_characters
test_cases.scenario: Analyze blog post with Unicode characters, emojis, and special characters in content, keywords, and meta description.

input.blog_post_content: SEO Optimization Guide 2024 – Complete Checklist 📚. Learn how to optimize your content for search engines. Keywords like 'SEO optimization' & 'keyword density' are essential. Meta descriptions (150-160 characters) summarize page content. Headers (H1, H2, H3) organize information. Internal links → distribute authority. External links → build credibility. Readability scores (0-100) measure clarity. Schema markup enables rich snippets ✨. Content freshness signals relevance. URL slugs should include keywords. Image alt-text improves accessibility. All factors contribute to SEO success. Regular audits identify opportunities. Optimization is ongoing. Quality content matters. User experience is critical. Technical SEO provides foundation. On-page optimization helps. Off-page signals matter. Backlinks build authority. Social signals indicate relevance. Engagement metrics show value. Conversion tracking measures success. Analytics reveal insights. Data drives decisions. Testing validates hypotheses. Iteration improves results.
input.meta_description: Complete SEO audit guide – keyword density, headers, links & readability. Optimize your blog posts for search engines 🚀

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill correctly handles Unicode characters and emojis. Keyword analysis counts variants with special characters. Meta description character count includes Unicode characters correctly. Readability metrics process special characters without errors. All phases complete successfully. Output preserves Unicode in recommendations and analysis.

test_cases.name: error_handling_missing_required_content
test_cases.scenario: Attempt to analyze with empty or null blog_post_content to trigger error handling for missing required input.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill aborts execution in Phase 1. Error message indicates content is empty or too short (<100 words). Response suggests user provide minimum 100-word draft. No partial results are returned. Clear error message guides user to correct input.

test_cases.name: error_handling_target_keywords_not_found
test_cases.scenario: Provide target keywords that do not appear in blog post content to test keyword mismatch handling.

input.blog_post_content: This blog post discusses content marketing strategies. We focus on audience engagement and brand building. Social media plays a key role. Email marketing drives conversions. Video content increases engagement. Storytelling creates emotional connections. User experience matters. Design affects perception. Navigation should be intuitive. Loading speed impacts rankings. Mobile optimization is essential. Responsive design works everywhere. Accessibility benefits all users. Color contrast improves readability. Typography enhances clarity. Whitespace reduces cognitive load. Consistency builds trust. Branding creates recognition. Voice and tone matter. Messaging resonates with audience. Value proposition is clear. Call-to-action is compelling. Conversion optimization increases sales. Analytics track performance. Testing validates improvements. Data informs decisions. Iteration refines approach. Continuous improvement drives growth.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill detects that target keywords do not appear in content. Phase 1 flags as critical issue: 'Target keywords not found in content.' Skill adjusts by inferring primary keywords from content frequency (likely 'content', 'marketing', 'engagement'). Audit continues with inferred keywords. Report recommends integrating target keywords naturally into content. Overall SEO score reflects missing keyword optimization.

test_cases.name: error_handling_conflicting_recommendations
test_cases.scenario: Analyze content where different audit phases produce conflicting recommendations (e.g., readability vs. keyword density) to test conflict resolution.

input.blog_post_content: SEO content audit SEO content audit SEO content audit. Keyword density optimization requires strategic keyword placement. SEO content audit appears frequently. The implementation of search engine optimization necessitates comprehensive keyword research and meticulous content analysis. SEO content audit is critical. Readability suffers when keyword density increases excessively. SEO content audit must balance optimization with user experience. The juxtaposition of keyword frequency and readability presents a dichotomous challenge requiring sophisticated resolution mechanisms. SEO content audit demands careful consideration. Simplifying sentences improves readability but may reduce keyword density. SEO content audit benefits from clear language. The multifaceted nature of content optimization requires balancing competing objectives. SEO content audit should prioritize user experience. Short sentences improve clarity but may not accommodate keyword variations. SEO content audit remains essential. Complex vocabulary demonstrates expertise but reduces accessibility. SEO content audit requires strategic decisions.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill detects conflicting recommendations: Phase 1 suggests increasing keyword density (currently ~4.5%, above optimal 1-3%), while Phase 5 suggests simplifying sentences and reducing complex vocabulary to improve readability. Phase 8 resolves conflict by prioritizing SEO impact (keyword optimization) over readability, but notes trade-off in report. Recommendations are ordered by impact: (1) reduce keyword density to 1.5-2%, (2) simplify sentences to improve readability, (3) document decision logic. Report explicitly states: 'Prioritized keyword optimization; readability can be improved through sentence simplification without sacrificing keyword placement.'

test_cases.name: quality_gate_keyword_density_boundary
test_cases.scenario: Analyze content with keyword density exactly at quality gate boundaries (0.5%, 1%, 3%, 5%) to test gate pass/fail conditions.

Optimization increases traffic. Optimization builds authority. Optimization enhances experience. Optimization drives conversions. Optimization matters greatly. Content quality is essential. Content optimization improves performance. Content freshness signals relevance. Content authority builds trust. Content engagement indicates value. Keywords are critical. Keywords drive discovery. Keywords match intent. Keywords appear naturally. Keywords vary appropriately. Keywords build relevance. Keywords improve rankings. Keywords guide strategy. Keywords inform decisions. Keywords measure success. Search engines rank content. Search engines understand intent. Search engines value quality. Search engines prefer freshness. Search engines reward engagement. Search engines recognize authority. Search engines index pages. Search engines crawl sites. Search engines process signals. Search engines deliver results.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill calculates keyword density for each target keyword. 'Optimization' appears ~10 times in ~200 words = 5% density (at upper boundary, triggers 'too-high' assessment). 'Content' appears ~10 times = 5% (too-high). 'Keywords' appears ~10 times = 5% (too-high). 'Search engines' appears ~10 times = 5% (too-high). Combined density ~20% (well above 10% gate threshold). Quality gate FAILS: 'Density values exceed optimal ranges; combined keyword density 20% exceeds 10% threshold.' Report flags as critical issue and recommends reducing keyword frequency.

test_cases.name: quality_gate_readability_target_audience_match
test_cases.scenario: Analyze content with readability metrics that barely pass or fail target audience expectations (general: 60-70, technical: 50-60, academic: 40-50).

input.blog_post_content: The multifaceted paradigm of search engine optimization encompasses a comprehensive constellation of interconnected variables. Algorithmic sophistication necessitates meticulous consideration of semantic relevance, lexical density, and syntactic complexity. The juxtaposition of keyword frequency and readability presents dichotomous challenges requiring sophisticated resolution mechanisms. Computational linguistics facilitate the analysis of textual characteristics. Morphological analysis reveals word structure. Syntactic parsing identifies sentence construction. Semantic analysis determines meaning. Pragmatic interpretation considers context. Discourse analysis examines relationships. Rhetorical analysis evaluates persuasiveness. Stylistic analysis assesses tone. Linguistic analysis provides comprehensive evaluation. The implementation of natural language processing enables automated content assessment. Machine learning algorithms identify patterns. Neural networks process information. Deep learning models improve accuracy. Artificial intelligence enhances capabilities. Computational efficiency optimizes performance. Scalability enables large-scale analysis. Robustness ensures reliability. Validity guarantees accuracy. Reliability ensures consistency. Generalizability extends applicability. The aforementioned considerations collectively constitute a comprehensive framework for content evaluation.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill calculates Flesch-Kincaid grade level (likely 14-16, college/graduate level) for content with complex vocabulary and long sentences. Target audience is 'general' (expects grade 8-10). Quality gate FAILS: 'Readability grade 15 exceeds target audience expectation (general = 8-10). Content is too complex for general audience.' Phase 5 identifies multiple long sentences (>20 words) and complex vocabulary items. Report recommends: (1) break long sentences into shorter ones, (2) replace complex words with simpler alternatives, (3) reduce average sentence length from ~25 words to ~17 words.

test_cases.name: quality_gate_header_hierarchy_invalid
test_cases.scenario: Analyze content with invalid header hierarchy (multiple H1s, H3 before H2, level skipping) to test hierarchy validation gate.

input.blog_post_content:
# SEO Content Audit Guide

This is the introduction.

### Image Alt-Text Best Practices

Images need descriptive alt-text.

## Keyword Optimization

Keyword density matters.

# Second H1 Header

This violates hierarchy rules.

## Meta Description Audit

Meta descriptions summarize content.

### Internal Linking Strategy

Internal links distribute authority.

## Readability Analysis

Readability affects user engagement.

#### Deep Nested Header

This skips H3 level.

## Schema Markup Implementation

Schema markup enables rich snippets.

### External Linking Best Practices

External links build credibility.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill parses headers and detects: (1) Two H1 headers (violates 'exactly one H1' rule), (2) H3 appears before H2 in first section (violates hierarchy), (3) H4 appears without H3 parent (level skipping). Quality gate FAILS: 'Header hierarchy invalid. Issues detected: Multiple H1s (2 found, 1 expected), H3 appears before H2 in section 'Image Alt-Text', H4 appears without H3 parent.' Phase 3 output includes specific hierarchy_issues array with each violation. Report recommends: (1) Remove second H1, (2) Promote 'Image Alt-Text' from H3 to H2, (3) Demote 'Deep Nested Header' from H4 to H3.

testable_surfaces: Inferred keywords (if target keywords not provided)

phases.phase: Phase 2: URL Slug and Meta Description Audit

critical_surfaces.surface: Keyword density quality gate (0-5% range, sum ≤10%)
Header hierarchy validation (no multiple H1s, no level skipping)
Readability target audience match (grade level within ±5 of target)
Error handling: missing required content (<100 words)
Error handling: target keywords not found in content
Overall SEO score calculation (0-100 with weighted sub-scores)
Improvement comparison (metric deltas from previous audit)

test_rationale.name: happy_path_complete_blog_post
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates the complete happy path workflow with all optional inputs provided. This is the most comprehensive test case and exercises all eight phases end-to-end.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Full skill execution with rich input data: complete blog post, target keywords, meta description, internal/external links, site inventory, dates, competitor URLs, and previous audit result. Tests all phases and outputs.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Would catch failures in: keyword analysis with competitive benchmarking, URL slug audit, meta description validation, header structure with competitive comparison, image alt-text audit, internal/external linking audits, readability analysis, schema markup validation, content freshness assessment, comprehensive report compilation with improvement comparison. Validates that all outputs are produced and contain expected data structures.

test_rationale.name: happy_path_minimal_inputs
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates graceful degradation when only required input (blog_post_content) is provided. Tests the skill's ability to handle sparse data and provide useful output despite missing optional inputs.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Skill behavior with minimal inputs: only blog_post_content and target_audience_level. Tests keyword inference (no target keywords provided), meta description generation (missing), URL slug audit skip (no URL), image alt-text skip (no images), generic internal linking recommendations (no site inventory), external linking skip (no external links), content freshness incomplete (no dates).
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Would catch failures in: keyword inference logic, graceful handling of missing optional inputs, appropriate flagging of skipped audits, generic recommendation generation, error handling for missing data. Validates that skill doesn't crash when optional inputs are absent and provides meaningful output despite limitations.

test_rationale.name: edge_case_short_content
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the boundary condition of minimum content length (100 words). Validates that the skill correctly processes content at the exact threshold and handles potential limitations of short content analysis.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Skill behavior with exactly 100-word blog post (minimum threshold). Tests keyword analysis with limited vocabulary, readability metrics with minimal data, and overall audit quality with short content. Validates that skill executes all phases but may note limitations.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Would catch failures in: word count validation, readability metric reliability with short content, keyword density calculation accuracy, phase execution with minimal data. Validates that skill doesn't reject content at exactly 100 words and provides appropriate caveats about analysis reliability.

test_rationale.name: edge_case_unicode_and_special_characters
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the skill's ability to handle international characters, emojis, and special characters in content and metadata. Validates proper character encoding and processing.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Skill behavior with Unicode characters (em-dash –), emojis (📚, 🚀, ✨), special characters (&, →, etc.) in blog content, keywords, meta description, and URL. Tests character counting, keyword matching, readability calculation, and output generation with non-ASCII characters.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Would catch failures in: Unicode character handling, emoji processing, special character encoding, character count accuracy (must include Unicode), keyword matching with special characters, readability metrics with non-ASCII text. Validates that skill correctly processes international content and special characters without corruption or errors.

test_rationale.name: error_handling_missing_required_content
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the error handling for missing or empty required input (blog_post_content). Validates that the skill aborts gracefully with clear error message rather than crashing or producing invalid output.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Skill behavior when blog_post_content is empty string or null. Tests Phase 1 entry criteria validation, error detection, and appropriate error response (Abort). Validates error message quality and user guidance.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Would catch failures in: input validation, error detection for empty content, appropriate error response (Abort vs. Adjust), error message clarity, prevention of partial/invalid output. Validates that skill follows error handling specification: 'Abort -- request minimum 100-word draft for meaningful analysis'.

test_rationale.name: error_handling_target_keywords_not_found
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the error handling for target keywords that don't appear in the blog post content. Validates that the skill detects the mismatch, flags it as a critical issue, and adjusts by inferring keywords.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Skill behavior when provided target keywords ('SEO optimization', 'keyword density', 'meta description') don't appear in blog content (which discusses 'content marketing', 'engagement', 'design'). Tests Phase 1 keyword matching, error detection, adjustment logic (infer keywords), and critical issue flagging.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Would catch failures in: keyword matching accuracy, error detection for missing keywords, appropriate error response (Adjust), keyword inference logic, critical issue flagging, recommendation generation for keyword integration. Validates that skill follows error handling specification: 'Adjust -- flag as critical issue; recommend keyword integration; continue with inferred keywords'.

test_rationale.name: error_handling_conflicting_recommendations
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the skill's ability to detect and resolve conflicting recommendations from different audit phases (e.g., readability vs. keyword density). Validates conflict resolution logic and decision documentation.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Skill behavior when Phase 1 (keyword analysis) recommends increasing keyword density while Phase 5 (readability) recommends simplifying sentences and reducing complex vocabulary. Tests conflict detection, prioritization logic (SEO impact > user experience), decision documentation, and recommendation ordering.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Would catch failures in: conflict detection, prioritization logic, decision documentation, recommendation ordering, trade-off explanation. Validates that skill follows error handling specification: 'Adjust -- prioritize by SEO impact score; if tied, prioritize user experience; document decision logic in report'.

test_rationale.name: quality_gate_keyword_density_boundary
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the quality gate for keyword density at boundary conditions (0.5%, 1%, 3%, 5%). Validates that the gate correctly identifies pass/fail conditions and density assessment accuracy.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Skill behavior with keyword density at or near quality gate boundaries. Content is crafted to produce specific density percentages for each keyword. Tests density calculation accuracy, assessment logic (optimal/too-high/too-low), and gate pass/fail determination.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Would catch failures in: keyword density calculation accuracy, boundary condition handling, assessment logic, gate pass/fail determination. Validates that skill correctly identifies when density exceeds optimal range (1-3% for primary, 0.5-1.5% for secondary) and flags as 'too-high' when >3%.

test_rationale.name: quality_gate_readability_target_audience_match
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the quality gate for readability metrics matching target audience expectations. Validates that the gate correctly assesses whether readability grade level matches audience level and flags mismatches.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Skill behavior with readability metrics that fail to match target audience expectations. Content is crafted to produce high Flesch-Kincaid grade (14-16, college/graduate level) for target audience 'general' (expects 8-10). Tests readability calculation, target audience matching, gate pass/fail determination, and rewrite suggestion generation.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Would catch failures in: Flesch-Kincaid grade calculation, target audience expectation validation, gate pass/fail determination, mismatch detection, rewrite suggestion generation. Validates that skill correctly identifies when readability grade exceeds target audience expectation and flags as gate failure with specific improvement recommendations.

test_rationale.name: quality_gate_header_hierarchy_invalid
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests the quality gate for header hierarchy validation. Validates that the gate correctly detects invalid hierarchy (multiple H1s, H3 before H2, level skipping) and flags structural issues.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Skill behavior with invalid header hierarchy: two H1 headers, H3 appearing before H2, H4 appearing without H3 parent. Tests header parsing, hierarchy validation, issue detection, gate pass/fail determination, and specific issue identification.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Would catch failures in: header parsing accuracy, hierarchy validation logic, multiple H1 detection, level skipping detection, H3 before H2 detection, gate pass/fail determination, specific issue identification. Validates that skill correctly identifies all hierarchy violations and provides specific recommendations for fixing each issue.
